keyTrack title misspelled; MB tracklist has 'Something for Liza' (Liza, not Lisa).

notes: MB search (sim 0.77): Chet Baker & Crew, 1956, Pacific Jazz — entity matches. 5 of 6 keyTracks match exactly; 'Something for Lisa' is a one-letter spelling error for MB 'Something for Liza'. Primary issue is the year (1993 vs 1956). Label 'Various' placeholder; MB shows Pacific Jazz.

The only MB match (search, sim 1.0) is a 2021 Compilation (20th Century Masterworks) crediting Stan Getz + Charlie Byrd; all 6 keyTracks (Desafinado, Samba Dees Days, O Pato, Samba Triste, Samba De Uma Nota Só, É Luxo Só) match its tracklist, so the entity/work is correct. The year claim 'Recorded in 2008' cannot be checked: the MB evidence is a 2021 reissue compilation, whose release date is NOT a recording date and is LATER than ours, so it does not contradict 2008. The original Jazz Samba (Getz/Byrd) is widely known to be 1962, but that is background knowledge, not in the pack evidence — so both '2008' and the comp's '2021' are unverifiable as recording dates. Repair-writer should source the true 1962 date externally. Label 'Various' placeholder.

MB embedded_mbid matches a Gerry Mulligan Live release titled 'California Concerts, Volume 2' (Pacific Jazz CD, 1988); all 6 keyTracks match its tracklist exactly, confirming the entity. Year 1988 agrees with the MB release/first-release date, so no contradiction — but note this is a Live CD reissue of 1950s material, so 'Recorded in 1988' really reflects the reissue date (recording date not in evidence; not flagged as error since evidence does not contradict). Title differs from MB by the missing ', Volume 2' edition designation — repair-writer should confirm whether the site means the original 'California Concerts' or this Vol. 2 release. Label 'Various' placeholder; MB shows Pacific Jazz.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Undercurrent is a 1962 jazz album by the pianist Bill Evans and the guitarist Jim Hall.

wrongClaims.problem: Year is wrong; Undercurrent is a 1962 album (recorded spring 1962). The MB release dated 2013 is a budget reissue compilation (Not Now Music); the entity-matched Wikipedia page dates the album to 1962.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: recorded in the spring of 1962, did he emerge again as a leader.

notes: Entity confirmed: the entity-matched Wikipedia page is 'Undercurrent (Bill Evans and Jim Hall album)', 1962, and all 6 keyTracks (My Funny Valentine, I Hear a Rhapsody, Dream Gypsy, Romain, Skating in Central Park, Darn That Dream) match the MB 2013-compilation tracklist. The MB release (2013, Compilation, Not Now Music) is a reissue — its date is not a recording date; the wiki establishes 1962, contradicting 'Recorded in 2013'. ours credits only 'Bill Evans' (album is Evans + Jim Hall) — per spec this is legitimate omission of a co-leader, NOT flagged. Year is the substantive error. Label 'Various' placeholder; MB shows Not Now Music.
